title: Weak Authentication Bypass
description: An attacker could potentially bypass authentication mechanisms, gaining unauthorized access to the system. This could occur due to weak password policies, lack of multi-factor authentication, or vulnerabilities in the authentication logic itself.  The absence of detailed authentication information makes assessing the risk extremely difficult.
attack_vector: Exploiting vulnerabilities in the authentication code, brute-force attacks, credential stuffing, or social engineering to obtain valid credentials.
mitigations: Implement strong password policies (length, complexity, rotation).
Enforce Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for all users.
Regularly audit authentication logs for suspicious activity and implement intrusion detection systems.
